T1027.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
T1027.description: Adversaries may attempt to make payloads difficult to discover or analyze by encrypting, encoding, or otherwise obfuscating its contents.

T1027.009.name: Embedded Payloads
T1027.009.description: Adversaries may embed payloads within other files to conceal malicious content.

T1036.008.name: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Resource
T1036.008.description: Adversaries may match the name or extension of a legitimate file to make their artifacts appear benign.

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands.

T1059.001.name: PowerShell
T1059.001.description: Adversaries may abuse PowerShell to perform actions and execute commands.

T1059.003.name: Windows Command Shell
T1059.003.description: Adversaries may abuse cmd.exe to execute commands.

T1059.004.name: Unix Shell
T1059.004.description: Adversaries may abuse Unix shell commands and scripts for execution.

T1059.006.name: Python
T1059.006.description: Adversaries may abuse Python for execution.

T1059.007.name: JavaScript
T1059.007.description: Adversaries may abuse JavaScript for execution.

T1071.name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.description: Adversaries may communicate using OSI application layer protocols.

T1071.001.name: Web Protocols
T1071.001.description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols associated with web traffic.

T1102.name: Web Service
T1102.description: Adversaries may use an existing, legitimate external Web service as a means for relaying C2 data.

T1140.name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information
T1140.description: Adversaries may use obfuscated files or information to hide artifacts of an intrusion.

T1552.001.name: Credentials In Files
T1552.001.description: Adversaries may search local file systems for files containing insecurely stored credentials.

T1053.003.name: Cron
T1053.003.description: Adversaries may abuse cron utilities to establish scheduled tasks for persistence.

T1547.001.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
T1547.001.description: Adversaries may achieve persistence by adding a program to a startup folder or run key.

T1567.002.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
T1567.002.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data to a cloud storage service.

T1571.name: Non-Standard Port
T1571.description: Adversaries may communicate using non-standard ports.

T1572.name: Protocol Tunneling
T1572.description: Adversaries may tunnel network communications to avoid detection.

T1562.001.name: Disable or Modify Tools
T1562.001.description: Adversaries may disable or modify security tools (AMSI, antivirus) to avoid detection.
